population: ICU patients with sepsis and lung infection (Sepsis 3.0 criteria, age 18–80 years)
sample_size: N=1676 (1191 survivors, 485 non-survivors; Training set n=1173, Validation set n=503)
predictor: Nomogram prediction model (including age, lactate, temperature, oxygenation index, BUN, GCS, liver disease, cancer, organ transplantation, Troponin T [TnT], neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio [NLR], CRRT, MV, and vasopressor use)
timing: Predictors measured within the first 24 hours of ICU admission
method: Retrospective cohort study using the MIMIC-III database; multivariable logistic regression for risk factor identification and nomogram construction; internal validation (7:3 split); comparison with SOFA and SAPS II scores
performance: Nomogram AUROC: 0.743 (95% CI: 0.713–0.773) in training set and 0.746 (95% CI: 0.699–0.790) in validation set; significantly higher than SOFA (0.647/0.596) and SAPS II (0.707/0.664). IDI and NRI also showed significant improvement over SOFA and SAPS II.
notes: Evidence extracted from text, Tables 1–4, and Figures 1–5. The nomogram demonstrated superior discrimination, calibration, and clinical net benefit (via Decision Curve Analysis) compared to traditional scoring systems for this specific population.
